reasoning: Primary tone selected from sector priority, sector tone hints, and brand tone hints.
Sector: Restaurant / Food Copy. Visual family: Food Warm Editorial.
Primary tone "warm-food-led" means: Use vivid but concrete food-led language anchored to real dishes, locality, and practical next steps.

contentRules.summary: Designed for food businesses that need appetite, local trust, and one truthful next step.
contentRules.closingStyle: Practical, visit-led, and calm rather than shouty.

blockRules.purpose: State cuisine, standout cue, and local context fast.
blockRules.audienceIntent: Decide in a few seconds whether this is worth visiting or trying.
structure: Lead with cuisine or standout dish cue.
Keep the sentence concrete and local.
forbiddenClaims: best in town
award-winning
authentic if not verified
open late if hours are missing
Do not invent hours, prices, offers, awards, lead times, or reservation support.
Do not claim a website, order flow, email route, or WhatsApp route unless verified.
Do not use unverified superlatives like best, leading, number one, or award-winning.
Do not fabricate team names, certifications, or years in business.
Do not promise online ordering, reservations, or a full menu URL when those routes are missing.
Do not say open now, open daily, late night, or list hours.
Do not say order online, checkout, or delivery now.
Do not imply a verified full external menu URL.
Do not present testimonial quotes or quote marks as if direct quotes exist.
degradationGuidance: If offer data is thin, keep the hero local and cuisine-led.
If order flow is missing, do not make the hero order-first.

blockRules.purpose: Explain what the place offers and the practical next step.
blockRules.audienceIntent: Understand service modes and whether the business is easy to visit.
structure: Name the place type or signature.
Mention one practical service or visit cue.
avoid: duplicating the headline word for word
forbiddenClaims: delivery promise if not confirmed
website route if missing
reservation support if not verified
Do not invent hours, prices, offers, awards, lead times, or reservation support.
Do not claim a website, order flow, email route, or WhatsApp route unless verified.
Do not use unverified superlatives like best, leading, number one, or award-winning.
Do not fabricate team names, certifications, or years in business.
Do not promise online ordering, reservations, or a full menu URL when those routes are missing.
Do not say open now, open daily, late night, or list hours.
Do not say order online, checkout, or delivery now.
Do not imply a verified full external menu URL.
Do not present testimonial quotes or quote marks as if direct quotes exist.
degradationGuidance: If service modes are missing, keep the line focused on location and category only.

blockRules.purpose: Add one useful, appetite-led support line under the hero.
blockRules.audienceIntent: Get a quick feel for why the business is worth trying.
forbiddenClaims: guaranteed quality
famous
best-loved
Do not invent hours, prices, offers, awards, lead times, or reservation support.
Do not claim a website, order flow, email route, or WhatsApp route unless verified.
Do not use unverified superlatives like best, leading, number one, or award-winning.
Do not fabricate team names, certifications, or years in business.

Do not say open now, open daily, late night, or list hours.

Do not imply a verified full external menu URL.
Do not present testimonial quotes or quote marks as if direct quotes exist.

avoid: button copy that explains too much

Do not present testimonial quotes or quote marks as if direct quotes exist.

blockRules.purpose: Support the main action without creating decision fatigue.
blockRules.audienceIntent: Keep a backup path close by.
structure: Do not repeat the primary CTA verb unless necessary.
avoid: more than two visible secondary actions
forbiddenClaims: website CTA if no site exists
delivery CTA if route is not verified

blockRules.purpose: Describe dishes, services, or products in a way that helps selection.

structure: Start with the item or category.
avoid: same sentence pattern for every card

spicy/hot level if not confirmed
pricing if not confirmed

structure: Keep the tone credible, not overexcited.
avoid: trying to sound like an ad testimonial

structure: Lead with the practical visit value.
Do not restate the full address multiple times in the same block.

walk-ins welcome if not verified

blockRules.purpose: Answer the most likely practical questions briefly.
blockRules.audienceIntent: Resolve first-visit friction without bloating the page.
structure: Questions should be real customer questions.
Answers should be short, direct, and factual.

reservation policy if not verified
team references if absent

contentOutlineBySector.reason: Move the user quickly from appetite and trust into ordering, visiting, or checking the menu.
Clarify the offer and the next step.
Keep the strongest truthful action near the top.
Help users scan the offer without overload.
Use the strongest trust proof available.
Location and contact must stay useful.
FAQ should reduce friction, not add noise.
Close on the strongest practical action.
Reinforce location and one trust cue only.

secondary.label: View Menu
guidance: Support the main action without competing with it.
The current page has enough structured offer content to support an in-page menu route.

secondary.label: Call
guidance: Support the main action without competing with it.

honestyRules: Only use what is verified or explicitly prepared in the brief.
Simplify instead of inventing when data is missing.
Prefer themes over quotes when testimonial quotes are absent.
Prefer local practical actions over unverified digital flows.

degradationCopyRules.guidance: Keep the copy visit-led or menu-led when no verified order flow exists.
Talk about menu highlights on-page instead of implying a complete external menu.
Do not mention open-now, opening windows, or easy late-night availability.
adjustments: stick to address and call cues

degradationCopyRules.guidance: Summarize review themes instead of writing quote-style proof.
If no exterior photo exists, keep the gallery dish-led and let location trust come from maps/contact blocks.

copyValidationRules.description: Each block should respect its configured word and character limits.
Copy must not imply unverified hours, prices, offers, awards, or booking capabilities.
Copy should avoid vague decorative language with no factual anchor.
CTA labels and supporting lines should not repeat the same action point excessively.
FAQ should answer practical questions with short, real answers, or stay absent.

issues.message: Repeated phrase across samples: "pickup or delivery".

checkedSamples.text: Crisp dosa and curry plates on Washington Street.
A Cork city stop for South Indian dosa, curry plates, dine-in, pickup, or delivery.
Start with the Paneer Potato Masala Dosa, then move to bhature when you want something heavier.
Get Directions
View Menu
Call
The clearest first order: a crisp dosa wrapped around paneer and potato masala.
Puffy bhature and chickpea curry when you want the richer, heavier plate.
Warm gulab jamun for a short sweet finish after the savoury plates.
4.6 rating, 610 reviews, and repeated praise for very tasty food and friendly staff.
Very tasty food
Friendly staff
Quick service
Dosa stands out
Real food photography with warm tabletop light and an honest local feel.
35 Washington St, Centre, Cork, T12 DHX2.
What should I try first?
Start with the Paneer Potato Masala Dosa. Go for Batura Chole Bhature if you want something heavier.
Can I call before pickup or delivery?
Yes. Use the phone number on the page if you want to confirm pickup or delivery details before you go.
Directions are the strongest truthful action in the current source set.
A Cork city stop for South Indian dosa, curry plates, dine-in, pickup, or delivery.
